methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found an official Toulouse Law School AI-in-law-teaching guideline page and PDF, a UT Capitole library teaching-support page, and several lower-specificity or non-policy results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ut-capitole.fr ("intelligences artificielles generatives" OR "IA generative") (bibliotheque OR BU OR citation OR sources OR donnees OR confidentialite OR RGPD)

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found no university-wide generative-AI policy page. It found an official Toulouse Law School charter/guidelines source for AI use in law teaching, assessment, data protection, and academic integrity; those claims are scoped to the law school. It also found a UT Capitole library teaching-support page for generative AI, which is useful as source-status/teaching-resource evidence but not as binding policy.

artifacts.claimText: An official Toulouse Law School page and PDF publish AI guidelines for law teaching; the source scopes the institutional AI-use framework to activities of teaching, training, and assessment within the Ecole de Droit de Toulouse, not to all Universite Toulouse Capitole programs.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices pour un bon usage de l'IA generative dans l'enseignement du droit
citation.publisher: Ecole de Droit de Toulouse, Universite Toulouse Capitole

artifacts.claimText: For Toulouse Law School teaching, training, and assessment activities, the AI guidelines say AI use by a student or teacher must be declared when it contributes to work that is submitted, published, or evaluated.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices pour un bon usage de l'IA generative dans l'enseignement du droit
citation.publisher: Ecole de Droit de Toulouse, Universite Toulouse Capitole

artifacts.claimText: The Toulouse Law School guidelines say homework may be corrected for pedagogical purposes but should not count toward continuous-assessment grades except where AI use itself is evaluated; in that case students must explain their method, describe AI use, and correct or analyze results.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices pour un bon usage de l'IA generative dans l'enseignement du droit
citation.publisher: Ecole de Droit de Toulouse, Universite Toulouse Capitole

artifacts.claimText: For Toulouse Law School students, the AI guidelines permit study-support uses such as summaries, explanations, plans, practice QCMs, and reformulation, but say AI must not be used to fabricate all or part of graded work and list generation of exam papers, homework, case comments, or dissertations as prohibited uses.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices pour un bon usage de l'IA generative dans l'enseignement du droit
citation.publisher: Ecole de Droit de Toulouse, Universite Toulouse Capitole

artifacts.claimText: The Toulouse Law School AI guidelines strongly discourage using institutional professional email addresses with commercial, foreign, or paid AI services; they invite users to use personal/dedicated accounts or university-secured AI tools, and prohibit depositing sensitive documents on external AI systems.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices pour un bon usage de l'IA generative dans l'enseignement du droit
citation.publisher: Ecole de Droit de Toulouse, Universite Toulouse Capitole

artifacts.claimText: The UT Capitole library maintains an official public page for teacher-researchers titled 'Enseigner dans le Superieur a l'ere des intelligences artificielles generatives,' describing an infographic intended to support informed use of generative-AI tools for learning quality.

artifacts.title: Universite Toulouse 1 Capitole staged AI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Official discovery found no university-wide AI policy page. Staged claims use Toulouse Law School's official AI guidelines and PDF, explicitly scoped to the law school, plus a separate UT Capitole library source-status claim for a teaching-support resource on generative AI.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The AI-specific rules found in this crawl are scoped to the Ecole de Droit de Toulouse unless a later official source establishes broader university-wide application.
The official UT Capitole library page is treated as a teaching-resource source, not as binding policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
